burial.plot_notes: Roselawn Memorial Gardens, Meadville Crawford County PA.

narrative_blocks.header: Ninety-six years in a single Pennsylvania county
narrative_blocks.body: Mary Elizabeth Curry was born February 5, 1884 in Meadville Pennsylvania and died there January 24, 1981 — ninety-six years in a single Pennsylvania county. She married Rush John Unger in 1906 and they were married for sixty years until his death in 1966. She outlived her husband by fifteen years.

burial.plot_notes: Roselawn Memorial Gardens, Meadville Crawford County PA.

narrative_blocks.header: Ninety-six years in a single Pennsylvania county
narrative_blocks.body: Mary Elizabeth Curry was born February 5, 1884 in Meadville Pennsylvania and died there January 24, 1981 — ninety-six years in a single Pennsylvania county. She married Rush John Unger in 1906 and they were married for sixty years until his death in 1966. She outlived her husband by fifteen years.

burial.plot_notes: Roselawn Memorial Gardens, Meadville Crawford County PA — with wife Mary Curry Unger.

marriages.spouse_research_notes: Mary Elizabeth Curry Unger (I00670, 1884-1981 age 96). Sons Robert Harmon Unger (1910-2011 age 101) + Harold Alison 'Tubby' Unger (1916-2002 WWII vet) to be built later.

narrative_blocks.header: Eldest child, twelve when father died young
narrative_blocks.body: Rush John Unger was born June 1, 1882 in Geneva Crawford County Pennsylvania to seventeen-year-old Florence Hannah Ellis and her young Minnesota-born husband Harmon Demmington Unger. He was twelve when his father died at thirty-six in 1894, leaving him the eldest of four siblings — the youngest just five — in his widowed mother's household. Florence remarried Reuben McFarland the following year.

narrative_blocks.header: Both spouses lived past eighty in Meadville
narrative_blocks.body: Rush married Mary Elizabeth Curry of Meadville in 1906 — they would be married for sixty years until his death in 1966. Mary outlived him by fifteen years and reached ninety-six. Their two sons would also live extraordinarily long: Robert reached one hundred and one, Harold reached eighty-five.

burial.plot_notes: Roselawn Memorial Gardens, Meadville Crawford County PA — with wife Rhea Smith Unger and parents Rush + Mary.

career.notes: Forty years at Talon (zipper/fastener manufacturer founded in Meadville), retiring 1973.
Owned and operated Unger's Antique Shop on Pennsylvania Avenue in the Kerrtown section of Meadville for many years.
Worked for Erie Railroad prior to Talon career.

naming_inspiration.description: Middle name Harmon honors his paternal grandfather Harmon Demmington Unger (1857-1894), the Minnesota-born settler who died at thirty-six leaving four young children — including Robert's father Rush — fatherless.

narrative_blocks.header: Both spouses centenarians, seventy-seven year marriage
narrative_blocks.body: Robert Harmon Unger lived to one hundred and one — and his wife Rhea Virgilia Smith outlived him to one hundred and three. Their seventy-seven-year marriage and joint century-plus lifespans were the apex of the Unger-Curry family longevity pattern. His mother Mary Curry had lived to ninety-six, his father Rush to eighty-four, his uncle Irvin to ninety-eight.

narrative_blocks.header: Forty years Talon engineering, owned antique shop
narrative_blocks.body: Robert retired from Talon's engineering department in 1973 after forty years of service. He had previously worked for Erie Railroad. He also owned and operated Unger's Antique Shop on Pennsylvania Avenue in Meadville's Kerrtown neighborhood for many years.

burial.plot_notes: Roselawn Memorial Gardens, Meadville Crawford County PA — with husband Robert Harmon Unger.

narrative_blocks.header: Lived to 103, both spouses past one hundred
narrative_blocks.body: Rhea Virgilia Smith lived to one hundred and three — her husband Robert Harmon Unger to one hundred and one. Their seventy-seven-year marriage covered nearly the entire span of both their long lives. Specific birth and death dates and her maiden family background remain undocumented in available sources.

military_service.notes: World War II veteran U.S. Army, Staff Sergeant. Specific unit, theater, and dates of service undocumented in available sources.

narrative_blocks.header: WWII Staff Sergeant, lived to eighty-five
narrative_blocks.body: Harold 'Tubby' Unger served in World War II as a Staff Sergeant in the U.S. Army. He lived to eighty-five, dying in 2002 — far short of his older brother Robert who would reach one hundred and one nine years later. Both brothers grew up in Meadville Pennsylvania to Rush Unger and Mary Curry.

burialCemetery.name: Roselawn Memorial Gardens
burialCemetery.notes: Roselawn Memorial Gardens, Meadville Crawford County PA. Burial site of multiple Unger and Lybarger descendants of Florence Hannah Ellis McFarland — including her son Rush John Unger and his wife Mary Curry Unger.
